title: The Pawn Chain
subtitle: Attack the base
summary: Read pawn chains by their direction, then attack the base instead of the protected head.
attribution: Original prose; ideas after Nimzowitsch, My System. No book text reproduced.

steps.title: Pawns point to plans
steps.markdown: A [[pawn-chain]] is a diagonal structure of pawns defending each other. Black's chain runs e6-d5-c4: e6 is the base, d5 supports c4, and c4 is the head. The base is usually easier to attack.

steps.title: Hit the base
steps.markdown: Black's chain points toward White's queenside space. Use the white f-pawn to capture the base of the chain.

hints.text: The base is the pawn farthest back in the chain.
Your f-pawn can capture on e6.

feedback.correct: Good. Capturing the base weakens the whole pawn chain.
feedback.wrong: Do not attack the protected head first. Look for the pawn at the base.

steps.title: Why the base?
steps.markdown: Why is the base of a pawn chain such an important target?

options.label: It supports the pawns in front of it
options.explain: Exactly. Remove the support and the advanced pawns become easier to challenge.

options.label: It is always the strongest pawn
options.explain: It may be important, but it is often vulnerable because it has fewer pawn defenders.

options.label: It lets every pawn move backward
options.explain: Pawns cannot move backward. That is why pawn structure creates long-term plans.

steps.title: Read the direction
steps.markdown: A pawn chain tells you where each side has space and where the structure can be undermined. Find the base, then build pressure there.
